arguments.title: Durable session workflow graph and handoff continuity
arguments.body:
<!-- aligned-structure:v2 -->

# Summary

Add a durable logical session workspace that carries pinned entities, an evolving execution roadmap, run lineage, and structured handoff state across agent runs.

## Motivation ("why")

Captured transcripts preserve evidence but do not give a resumed agent an authoritative roadmap. A session needs durable attention state and a mutable workflow that survives handoff without replaying the transcript or copying ticket state into a second source of truth.

## Dependent expectation

If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on one durable `workspace_session_id` spanning multiple capture `run_id` values; a persisted workflow containing ticket-backed and session-only nodes; live resolution of ticket state; structured handoff records; terminal and Mermaid rendering; and an explicit finish operation that succeeds only when required nodes and validation gates are satisfied.

## Guards

- `val-session-workflow-persistence`: workflow mutations and run lineage survive store reloads.
- `val-session-workflow-rendering`: terminal and Mermaid views are deterministic and include ticket and temporary nodes.
- `val-session-handoff-continuity`: handoff persists the workspace session id and a new run resumes it without reusing the outgoing run id.
- `val-session-workflow-finish`: finish rejects incomplete required nodes or failed/missing required validation and accepts a complete graph.

## Positions

- Runtime session capture and transcript store: `implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`.
- Runtime session model: `partial` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs`; capture/run models exist, but durable workspace context and workflow models do not.
- Workflow persistence and mutation: `not-implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/workflow.rs`.
- Terminal and Mermaid rendering: `not-implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/workflow_render.rs`.
- Structured handoff/resume: `not-implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/handoff.rs`.
- CLI and MCP workflow surfaces: `not-implemented` at `memory-api/tools/cli/session-cli/src/lib.rs` and `memory-api/tools/mcp/session-mcp/src/server.rs`.

## Governing-rule requirement

This contract is governed by the repository spec-system guidance and its aligned-structure v2 requirement in `.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md`.

# Contract

## Identity and lineage

- `workspace_session_id` identifies the durable logical workspace and is reused across handoffs.
- Every agent execution has a distinct `run_id` and may reference a `predecessor_run_id`.
- A receiving run resumes the same `workspace_session_id`; it does not reuse the outgoing `run_id`.

## Workflow model

- Ticket nodes persist authoritative ticket URNs plus cached display metadata; current ticket state is resolved live when viewing or finishing.
- Session-only nodes represent actions, decisions, checkpoints, or validation work discovered during execution.
- Nodes have stable IDs, required/optional classification, status, and timestamps.
- Directed edges express execution order or dependency. Agents may add nodes and edges as work is discovered.
- Promotion of a session-only node to a ticket preserves the session node identity and records the resulting ticket URN.

## Persistence

- Workflow state is stored separately from transcript capture so each schema can evolve independently.
- Mutations are file-backed and flushed before returning.
- Re-initialization and resume are idempotent and do not discard pins, graph nodes, edges, run lineage, or handoff records.

## Rendering

- Terminal output provides a deterministic, compact dependency view with node status and required/optional markers.
- Mermaid output emits a deterministic `flowchart` representation with safely escaped labels.
- Rendering does not mutate workflow state.

## Handoff and resume

- Handoff persists a structured record before rendering any prompt.
- The record includes `workspace_session_id`, outgoing `run_id`, handoff ID, pins, workflow status, blockers, validation state, and an exact resume command.
- `/handoff` always includes the durable workspace session ID and instructs the receiving session to resume it with a new run ID.

## Finish

- Finish is explicit and idempotent.
- It is rejected while any required node is incomplete or required validation is not passing.
- Optional nodes may remain incomplete only when explicitly deferred with a reason.
- Successful finish records completion time and final run ID without deleting context or workflow history.

# Non-goals

- Copying ticket lifecycle state into the session store.
- Requiring feedback-api for context or workflow persistence.
- Semantic auto-pinning or inferred ticket edges.
- Replacing the ticket graph as the authoritative durable work graph.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. A workspace session can be initialized, mutated, reloaded, handed off, and resumed under the same `workspace_session_id` with distinct linked run IDs.
2. Ticket-backed and session-only nodes can be added, updated, linked, and promoted without duplicate identity.
3. Ticket state is resolved live; stale or unavailable references produce diagnostics without corrupting the workflow.
4. Terminal and Mermaid renders are deterministic and represent the same nodes, edges, and statuses.
5. Handoff persistence occurs before prompt rendering and every rendered handoff includes the durable ID and exact resume command.
6. Finish enforces required nodes and validation gates and records terminal success.
7. Feedback usage/rating emission is optional through an injected sink and cannot block context or workflow mutation.

# Traceability

- Parent spec: `memory-api/session-api/dynamic-session-bootstrapping` (`8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`).
- Related runtime context spec: `memory-api/session-api/runtime-session-context` (`709f067a-21b6-41b6-8879-3cacef4bacaf`).
- Related handoff prompt spec: `context-engine/handoff-workflow-prompts` (`9e04ff58-9160-4766-b307-74c0fb32a92c`).

turns.content: The owning spec is now [c677182e Durable session workflow graph and handoff continuity](.spec/specs/c677182e-90da-4ac3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf/spec.toml). I’m opening three focused implementation tickets in the `memory-api` store: workflow persistence/mutation, deterministic renderers, and structured handoff/resume; after creation I’ll wire dependencies and update the existing runtime and transport tickets.

arguments.title: [session-api] Durable session workflow persistence and mutation
arguments.description:
# Goal

Implement the durable logical session workspace and mutable workflow graph defined by spec `memory-api/session-api/durable-session-workflow` (`c677182e-90da-4ac3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf`).

## Scope

- Add a durable `workspace_session_id` distinct from per-agent capture `run_id` values.
- Persist workflow state separately from transcript capture.
- Support ticket-backed nodes containing authoritative URNs plus cached display metadata.
- Support session-only action, decision, checkpoint, and validation nodes.
- Add stable node IDs, required/optional classification, statuses, timestamps, and directed dependency/order edges.
- Support add/update/link/remove-safe operations and promotion of a temporary node to a ticket URN.
- Resolve ticket state live when reading or finishing; do not copy ticket lifecycle state into session storage.
- Flush every mutation before returning and preserve graph state across idempotent init/resume.
- Keep feedback emission optional through an injected sink.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. A workflow can be initialized, mutated, reloaded, and resumed without losing nodes, edges, pins, or run lineage.
2. A new run resumes the same `workspace_session_id` with a distinct `run_id` and optional predecessor.
3. Ticket and temporary nodes coexist and promotion preserves identity.
4. Missing ticket references return per-node diagnostics without corrupting persisted state.
5. Focused `session-api` tests cover persistence, idempotency, mutation, promotion, and live-state adapter behavior.

## Depends on

- Runtime pinned-context foundation `412964a3-e1c3-47da-94ad-268ff20441c0`.

## Spec

`c677182e-90da-4ac3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf`.

# Goal

Render the durable session workflow as a compact terminal dependency graph or deterministic Mermaid flowchart.

## Scope

- Add a terminal renderer with node status, required/optional marker, live ticket state, and dependency order.
- Add deterministic Mermaid `flowchart` rendering with escaped labels and stable node IDs.
- Ensure both formats represent the same nodes, edges, statuses, blockers, and completion state.
- Return diagnostics for unresolved ticket URNs without mutating workflow state.
- Expose renderer functions for later CLI/MCP transport wiring.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Terminal and Mermaid output is deterministic across repeated renders.
2. Both formats include ticket-backed and session-only nodes and all persisted edges.
3. Labels are safely escaped and graph output remains syntactically valid for representative titles.
4. Rendering does not change persisted workflow bytes.
5. Focused snapshot or exact-output tests cover branching, blocked, optional, completed, and unresolved-node cases.

## Depends on

- Durable workflow persistence and mutation ticket created in this planning update.

## Spec

`c677182e-90da-4ac3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf`.

# Goal

Persist structured session handoffs and resume the same durable workspace under a new capture run.

## Scope

- Persist a handoff record before rendering the next-session prompt.
- Include `workspace_session_id`, outgoing `run_id`, handoff ID, pins, workflow status, blockers, validation state, and exact resume command.
- Resume the same `workspace_session_id` with a new `run_id` linked to the outgoing/predecessor run.
- Add explicit, idempotent finish behavior gated by required workflow nodes and validation outcomes.
- Preserve completed context, workflow, handoff, and run lineage as history.
- Provide core APIs consumed by session CLI/MCP and generated `/handoff` guidance.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Handoff persistence completes before prompt rendering.
2. Every handoff payload includes the durable workspace ID, outgoing run ID, handoff ID, and exact resume command.
3. Resume rejects accidental reuse of the outgoing run ID and records predecessor linkage.
4. Finish rejects incomplete required nodes or missing/failed required validation.
5. Finish accepts a complete graph and is idempotent.
6. Focused tests cover handoff/resume continuity, crash-safe ordering, and finish gates.

## Depends on

- Durable workflow persistence and mutation ticket created in this planning update.

## Specs

- `c677182e-90da-4ac3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf`.

# session-api: runtime cognitive-workspace foundation

Extend `session-api` from a write/archive store into the durable read/runtime foundation used by pinned context and the session workflow.

## Decisions baked in

- A durable `workspace_session_id` identifies the logical workspace across handoffs.
- Each agent execution uses a distinct `run_id` with optional predecessor linkage; handoff reuses the workspace ID, not the outgoing run ID.
- Pinned entities are cross-store URNs.
- Context mutations are file-backed and flushed before returning.

arguments.description:
# Epic: Dynamic Session Bootstrapping and Durable Workflow Context

Redesign agent startup and continuation around a durable logical session workspace rather than always-on static context or transcript replay.

## Product contract

A workspace session carries:

- a durable `workspace_session_id` spanning handoffs;
- distinct linked capture `run_id` values per agent execution;
- pinned ticket, spec, and rule URNs;
- a mutable workflow graph containing ticket-backed and session-only nodes;
- live ticket-state resolution;
- terminal and Mermaid roadmap rendering;
- structured handoff records and exact resume commands;
- explicit graph-gated finish.

Feedback usage/rating emission is an optional integration and cannot block context or workflow persistence.

## Sequenced roadmap

1. **Design and addressing foundations — done.** Frozen bootstrap decisions `afa00b5c`; URN resolver `82d6ada4`; multi-store discovery `6bd67a7a`.
2. **Runtime pinned context — next implementation leaf.** `412964a3` adds durable workspace identity, run lineage, pin/unpin/read/view, and optional feedback sink behavior.
3. **Durable workflow core.** `70cd7056` persists and mutates ticket-backed plus session-only workflow nodes and edges.
4. **Parallel workflow consumers.** `cc4b0289` renders terminal/Mermaid graphs; `0647a212` persists handoffs, resumes new runs, and enforces finish gates.
5. **CLI/MCP surfaces.** `6b2dc497` exposes context, workflow, rendering, handoff/resume, and finish commands.
6. **Generated handoff guidance.** `9577b114` requires every `/handoff` to carry the durable ID and exact resume flow.
7. **Cascade auto-discovery.** `d8f76965` remains optional follow-up work blocked on structured hard links `b03be2d5` and `f00291a3`; manual pin/workflow commands do not wait for it.
8. **Minimal selective loading.** `b4a8dc5e` follows the usable CLI/MCP context surface.

## Planning and review cleanup

- Parent contract: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
- Durable workflow contract: `c677182e-90da-4ac3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf`.
- Runtime and handoff child specs must use aligned-structure v2 and accurate implementation positions.
- Core feedback `c7542933` has implementation and focused validation recorded and should complete independent review; ingestion/full feedback work is outside this epic's critical path.

## Done when

The session API can initialize or resume a durable workspace, manage pinned entities and an evolving roadmap, render the roadmap in terminal or Mermaid form, persist a structured handoff, resume under a new linked run, and reject finish until required work and validation gates are satisfied. Generated `/handoff` guidance always carries the durable workspace ID and exact resume command.

<!-- aligned-structure:v2 -->

# Summary

Turn `session-api` from capture/archive-only storage into a durable runtime workspace with selective pinned context, an evolving workflow roadmap, structured handoff continuity, and explicit completion gates.

## Motivation ("why")

Always-on instruction loading and transcript replay consume model context without telling a resumed agent what work remains. Durable session state must preserve active knowledge and execution intent while keeping ticket state authoritative in the ticket store.

## Dependent expectation

If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on a durable `workspace_session_id` spanning distinct linked run IDs; pinned entity URNs; a mutable ticket-backed workflow; terminal and Mermaid views; structured handoff/resume; and graph-gated finish.

## Guards

- `val-session-bootstrap-schema-validation`.
- `val-session-init-idempotency`.
- `val-session-workflow-persistence`.
- `val-session-handoff-continuity`.
- `val-session-workflow-finish`.

## Positions

- Capture/archive store: `implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`.
- Capture models: `implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs`.
- Runtime pinned context: `not-implemented`; planned by ticket `412964a3-e1c3-47da-94ad-268ff20441c0`.
- Durable workflow persistence: `not-implemented`; planned by ticket `70cd7056-c342-4433-ad60-5bc798f61aa6`.
- Terminal/Mermaid rendering: `not-implemented`; planned by ticket `cc4b0289-b6fd-412f-a97a-497f05f572f4`.
- Structured handoff/resume and finish: `not-implemented`; planned by ticket `0647a212-9d2e-4943-9627-f854ce3f14c4`.
- CLI/MCP bootstrap surfaces: `not-implemented`; planned by ticket `6b2dc497-188c-44f5-9106-bf35deecb7a1`.
- Hard-link cascade: `not-implemented`; planned by ticket `d8f76965-1ff3-4a0a-bb24-773b9637fae4`.

## Governing-rule requirement

This contract is governed by `.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md` and its aligned-structure v2 requirement.

# Contract

- Client-side rendering consumes structured session views; runtime code does not rewrite instruction files.
- File-backed mutations flush before returning.
- Entity references use cross-store URNs and hard links; semantic search never silently auto-pins.
- `workspace_session_id` is durable across handoff; every execution creates a distinct `run_id` with predecessor linkage.
- Pinned context and workflow state survive resume without transcript replay.
- Ticket nodes resolve current state live; session-only nodes may represent discovered actions and later be promoted to tickets.
- Handoff records persist before prompt rendering and always supply an exact resume command.
- Finish is explicit and requires all required nodes and validation gates to be satisfied.
- Feedback usage/rating emission is optional through an adapter and cannot block context or workflow operations.

# Non-goals

- Replacing ticket-api as workflow authority.
- Copying ticket lifecycle state into session files.
- Gating core session functionality on the full feedback program.
- Semantic auto-pinning from vague text matches.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. Durable context initializes and resumes idempotently under one workspace ID across distinct runs.
2. Pinned entities and workflow nodes/edges persist independently of transcript capture.
3. Agents can add discovered work during execution and promote temporary nodes to tickets.
4. Terminal and Mermaid views deterministically represent the same roadmap.
5. Handoff always persists and carries workspace ID, run lineage, blockers, validation state, and exact resume command.
6. Finish rejects incomplete required work and accepts a validated complete workflow.
7. Existing capture artifacts remain backward compatible.

# Traceability

- Epic: `effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8`.
- Runtime context child: `709f067a-21b6-41b6-8879-3cacef4bacaf`.
- Durable workflow child: `c677182e-90da-4ac3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf`.
- Cascade child: `fda5c915-5c37-432f-acf1-8b20c6219fdc`.
- Selective-loading child: `a28a88db-2b41-49d2-897c-6dbcdb313255`.

# Summary

Add the durable runtime context foundation for pinned entities and workspace/run identity without disturbing capture/archive storage.

## Motivation ("why")

A transcript does not preserve active attention state in a compact, mutable form. Agents need to resume the same logical workspace, retain selected entities, and start a distinct linked run without replaying prior turns.

## Dependent expectation

If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on idempotent workspace initialization/resume, durable pinned entity URNs, distinct linked run IDs, immediate file-backed persistence, headers-only views, and optional non-blocking feedback emission.

## Guards

- `val-session-bootstrap-schema-validation`.
- `val-session-init-idempotency`.
- `val-session-context-capture-isolation`.
- `val-session-run-lineage`.

## Positions

- Existing capture models: `implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs`.
- Existing capture persistence: `implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`.
- Runtime context model and operations: `not-implemented`; ticket `412964a3-e1c3-47da-94ad-268ff20441c0`.

## Governing-rule requirement

This contract is governed by `.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md` and its aligned-structure v2 requirement.

# Contract

- `workspace_session_id` identifies a durable logical workspace.
- Every agent execution has a distinct `run_id` and optional `predecessor_run_id`.
- Initialization is load-or-create and never clobbers pins or lineage.
- Pinned tickets, specs, and rules are cross-store URNs with relation/reason metadata.
- Mutations flush before returning.
- Read/view returns short headers and metadata, never full entity bodies.
- Duplicate pin and missing unpin are idempotent no-ops.
- Feedback usage emission is optional through an injected sink; sink absence or failure cannot corrupt or reject a successful pin.
- Existing capture manifests and transcripts remain byte-identical when context mutates, and context remains byte-identical when capture persists.

# Non-goals

- Workflow graph persistence, rendering, handoff, and finish, owned by `c677182e-90da-4ac3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf`.
- Cascade auto-pinning.
- CLI/MCP transport.
- Full-body entity resolution.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. Fresh initialization creates durable context with workspace ID, schema version, timestamps, empty pins, and initial run lineage.
2. Resume preserves pins and adds a distinct linked run without reusing the outgoing run ID.
3. Pin/unpin is idempotent and immediately persistent.
4. Headers-only view never includes full bodies.
5. Optional feedback sink receives successful pin usage when configured and cannot block persistence.
6. Capture/context byte-isolation regressions pass.
7. Focused `cargo test -p session-api` coverage validates the contract.

# Traceability

- Parent spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
- Implementation ticket: `412964a3-e1c3-47da-94ad-268ff20441c0`.
- Downstream workflow ticket: `70cd7056-c342-4433-ad60-5bc798f61aa6`.
- Downstream transport ticket: `6b2dc497-188c-44f5-9106-bf35deecb7a1`.

# Summary

Define generated `/handoff` and `/handoff-tickets` prompts that persist and render compact, reference-centric handoffs for durable session workspaces.

## Motivation ("why")

Conversation summaries alone do not provide stable session identity or an authoritative restart point. Every handoff must carry the durable workspace, run lineage, roadmap status, and exact resume operation without replaying raw transcripts.

## Dependent expectation

If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on `/handoff` persisting a structured handoff record before rendering; always emitting `workspace_session_id`, outgoing `run_id`, handoff ID, and exact resume command; and instructing the receiver to reuse the workspace ID with a new linked run ID.

## Guards

- `val-session-handoff-continuity`.
- `val-handoff-prompt-rule-sync`.
- `val-handoff-reference-completeness`.

## Positions

- Existing generated handoff prompt: `partial` at `.agents/prompts/handoff.prompt.md`; it summarizes context but does not require durable session identity or persisted handoff input.
- Existing generated handoff-tickets prompt: `partial` at `.agents/prompts/handoff-tickets.prompt.md`.
- Durable handoff core: `not-implemented`; ticket `0647a212-9d2e-4943-9627-f854ce3f14c4`.
- Prompt contract update: `not-implemented`; ticket `9577b114-ec11-431b-8740-c488bef05fc9`.

## Governing-rule requirement

Generated prompt changes are governed by canonical rule entries and `rule-targets/30-agents-prompts.yaml`; generated files are never edited directly.

# Contract

- Handoff persists a structured record before rendering prompt text.
- Every output includes durable workspace ID, outgoing run ID, handoff-record ID, exact resume command, pinned entities, workflow status, blockers, and required validation state.
- The receiver reuses `workspace_session_id` and creates a distinct new `run_id` linked to the outgoing run.
- Raw transcript content remains a pointer unless a finding cannot be represented durably.
- `/handoff-tickets` follows the same identity/resume contract and creates or matches durable ticket work without duplication.
- Outputs remain compact, reference-centric, and compliant with clickable reference policy.

# Non-goals

- Implementing session persistence in generated prompt text.
- Reusing one capture run ID across multiple agent executions.
- Replaying full transcripts by default.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. Both generated prompts are sourced from canonical rule entries and pass synchronization checks.
2. `/handoff` always contains the durable ID and exact resume command from a persisted handoff record.
3. Receiver instructions create a new linked run rather than reusing the outgoing run ID.
4. Roadmap status, blockers, pins, and validation state are represented compactly.
5. All entity references resolve and no placeholder identity remains undeclared.

# Traceability

- Durable workflow spec: `c677182e-90da-4ac3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf`.
- Core handoff ticket: `0647a212-9d2e-4943-9627-f854ce3f14c4`.

- Original generated prompt ticket: `46d89aa2-043a-4c94-8213-2f365aa2d517`.

# Summary

Add a durable logical session workspace that carries pinned entities, an evolving execution roadmap, run lineage, and structured handoff state across agent runs.

## Motivation ("why")

Captured transcripts preserve evidence but do not give a resumed agent an authoritative roadmap. A session needs durable attention state and a mutable workflow that survives handoff without replaying the transcript or copying ticket state into a second source of truth.

## Dependent expectation

If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on one durable `workspace_session_id` spanning multiple capture `run_id` values; a persisted workflow containing ticket-backed and session-only nodes; live resolution of ticket state; structured handoff records; terminal and Mermaid rendering; and explicit graph-gated finish.

## Guards

- `val-session-workflow-persistence`.
- `val-session-workflow-rendering`.
- `val-session-handoff-continuity`.
- `val-session-workflow-finish`.

## Positions

- Runtime session capture and transcript store: `implemented` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`.
- Runtime session model: `partial` at `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs`.
- Workflow persistence and mutation: `not-implemented`; ticket `70cd7056-c342-4433-ad60-5bc798f61aa6`.
- Terminal and Mermaid rendering: `not-implemented`; ticket `cc4b0289-b6fd-412f-a97a-497f05f572f4`.
- Structured handoff/resume and finish: `not-implemented`; ticket `0647a212-9d2e-4943-9627-f854ce3f14c4`.
- CLI/MCP surfaces: `not-implemented`; ticket `6b2dc497-188c-44f5-9106-bf35deecb7a1`.

## Governing-rule requirement

This contract is governed by `.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md` and its aligned-structure v2 requirement.

# Contract

## Identity and lineage

- `workspace_session_id` identifies the durable workspace and is reused across handoffs.
- Every execution receives a distinct `run_id` and optional `predecessor_run_id`.

## Workflow model

- Ticket nodes persist authoritative ticket URNs plus cached display metadata; current state resolves live.
- Session-only nodes represent actions, decisions, checkpoints, or validation work discovered during execution.
- Nodes have stable IDs, required/optional classification, status, and timestamps.
- Directed edges express dependency or execution order and may be added during execution.
- Promotion to a ticket preserves the session node identity and records the resulting ticket URN.

## Persistence and rendering

- Workflow state is stored separately from transcript capture and flushed per mutation.
- Terminal and Mermaid renders are deterministic, equivalent, safely escaped, and read-only.

## Handoff and finish

- Handoff persists before rendering and includes workspace ID, outgoing run, handoff ID, pins, roadmap state, blockers, validation state, and exact resume command.
- Finish is explicit and idempotent; required nodes and validation must pass.
- Optional nodes may remain incomplete only when explicitly deferred with a reason.

# Non-goals

- Copying ticket lifecycle state into the session store.
- Requiring feedback-api for context or workflow persistence.
- Semantic auto-pinning or replacing the ticket graph.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. A workspace initializes, mutates, reloads, hands off, and resumes under the same workspace ID with distinct linked runs.
2. Ticket-backed and session-only nodes can be added, updated, linked, and promoted without duplicate identity.
3. Ticket state resolves live; unavailable references produce diagnostics without corruption.
4. Terminal and Mermaid renders deterministically represent the same graph.
5. Handoff persistence precedes rendering and always provides exact resume flow.
6. Finish enforces required work and validation and records terminal success.
7. Feedback emission is optional and non-blocking.

# Traceability

- Parent spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
- Runtime context spec: `709f067a-21b6-41b6-8879-3cacef4bacaf`.

- Transport ticket: `6b2dc497-188c-44f5-9106-bf35deecb7a1`.
- Prompt update ticket: `9577b114-ec11-431b-8740-c488bef05fc9`.

turns.content: The first pass already exposes a few contract-level failures despite green tests: finish does not consult live ticket state, transport never supplies a resolver, and missing validation can pass because callers provide the entire gate list. I’m narrowing those paths and checking persistence/URN validation before ranking the findings.

The tests are doing assertion theater around the happy path: there is no test named by the schema guard, no feedback-sink test, and the recorded runtime executions are absent from the working tree even though specs cite them. I’m checking the helper’s durability and the exact evidence deletions before closing the review.

**Findings**

1. **Critical: `finish` is a caller-certified success button.**  
[store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L1205) accepts the complete validation list from the caller and only checks entries supplied in that list. Both [session-cli](memory-api/tools/cli/session-cli/src/lib.rs#L477) and [session-mcp](memory-api/tools/mcp/session-mcp/src/server.rs#L681) default validation to an empty vector. Therefore, a caller can omit every required guard and successfully finish an otherwise completed graph. The contract says missing required validation must block; the implementation lets the defendant write the verdict.

2. **Critical: “live ticket-state finish” is not implemented.**  
`finish_workflow` reads persisted node statuses directly and has no ticket-state resolver parameter at all ([store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L1221)). Worse, every production transport explicitly passes `None` to rendering and handoff resolution ([session-cli](memory-api/tools/cli/session-cli/src/lib.rs#L468), [session-mcp](memory-api/tools/mcp/session-mcp/src/server.rs#L640)). Live resolution exists only as an injectable trait exercised by a mock. The tickets were closed around a decorative interface that no real command uses.

3. **High: dependency blockers are calculated backwards.**  
The test establishes `node-action -> node-ticket` as “action depends on ticket” ([store_tests.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs#L1014)). The renderer instead checks whether `edge.from` is incomplete and records it as a blocker of `edge.to` ([store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L997-L1013)). Thus the dependency is reported as blocked by its dependent. The roadmap confidently points in the wrong direction, which is an impressive achievement for a graph feature.

4. **High: persisted handoff data is immediately hidden from consumers.**  
The record contains pins, workflow, diagnostics, and validation, but `render_handoff_terminal` emits only IDs, the resume command, node/edge counts, and diagnostic codes ([store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L1164-L1184)). CLI and MCP return only that reduced string. No record path or structured record is returned. Consequently, generated guidance cannot obtain the promised pins, blockers, validation state, or finish readiness through the implemented transport.

5. **High: “durable” persistence is a truncate-and-pray operation.**  
Every context mutation and handoff eventually uses direct `fs::write` ([store_helpers.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_helpers.rs#L3-L15)). There is no temporary file, flush, atomic rename, lock, or recovery journal. A crash or concurrent writer can leave truncated JSON or silently discard another mutation. Calling this crash-safe durable state is architectural cosplay with a filesystem API.

6. **High: the validation trail does not prove what it claims.**  
[val-session-bootstrap-schema-validation.json](.test/default/specs/val-session-bootstrap-schema-validation.json#L3) runs the filter `context_schema`, but no such test exists. [val-session-run-lineage.json](.test/default/specs/val-session-run-lineage.json#L3) runs `run_lineage`, which also matches no test. Cargo treats zero matched tests as success. Meanwhile, the workflow spec cites several `exec-val-session-*` records ([body.md](.spec/specs/c677182e-90da-4ac3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf/body.md#L24-L27)), but none of those files currently exist. The root diff also deletes unrelated historical execution evidence. This is not traceability; it is paperwork wearing a test badge.

7. **Medium: URN validation accepts malformed and incorrectly typed references.**  
[store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L1518-L1544) never verifies that workspace or entity segments are non-empty. Values such as `ce:///tickets/id` and `ce://default/tickets/` pass. Workflow ticket nodes also accept spec or rule URNs because node kind is never checked against parsed URN kind ([store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L803-L837)). The parser verifies roughly the shape of a URL and then declares semantic victory.

8. **Medium: the parent spec still says completed work is unimplemented.**  
The parent positions continue to mark runtime context, workflow persistence, rendering, handoff, and transports as `not-implemented` ([body.md](.spec/specs/8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913/body.md#L25-L31)). Closing five tickets while leaving the owning contract stale violates the repository’s traceability rules and makes the epic’s status unreliable.

9. **Medium: the CLI does not implement the documented command hierarchy.**  
The ticket promises `session workflow add-ticket|add-step|update|link|promote|show|finish`, but the code adds flat variants such as `workflow-add-node`, `workflow-add-edge`, and separate terminal/Mermaid commands ([lib.rs](memory-api/tools/cli/session-cli/src/lib.rs#L68-L97)). It compiles, certainly. So would a completely different CLI.

**Open Questions**

- Are deleted unrelated execution records intentional? If not, the test-store recording operation has caused evidence loss and must be repaired before anything is committed.
- Should a finished workspace become immutable, or may later mutations invalidate its persisted finish record? The current code allows mutations while subsequent `finish` calls return the stale original record.

**Verdict**

Do not proceed to action 8 or close the epic. The implementation has useful model scaffolding, but the core guarantees are not actually enforced through production transports, durability is non-atomic, and validation evidence is unreliable.

Highest-leverage fix: make finish and handoff consume authoritative resolver/validation providers inside `session-api`, wire real implementations through CLI/MCP, then add adversarial tests for omitted validation, stale live ticket state, dependency direction, malformed URNs, crash-safe writes, and complete handoff output.

turns.content: First I’m isolating the nested `memory-api` patch from root-store/spec changes. The discriminating checks are whether authority moved inside `session-api`, whether the fallback resolver can actually find the correct ticket store, and whether the “atomic” writer survives Windows rename semantics instead of merely looking respectable in a diff.

The first pass fixed several visible defects, but two authority holes are still alive: supplied validation outcomes can override the test store, and a ticket node marked locally `done` bypasses its live ticket state. I’m pinning those to exact lines now, alongside the Windows write path and evidence-store status, because these are release blockers rather than naming-polish trivia.

The remediation clears the blocker-direction, structured-handoff, URN-shape, filter-name, and parent-position findings. What remains is uglier because it sits behind green tests: the finish path still trusts a caller’s forged `passed`, live ticket state still loses to cached `Done`, and the Windows “atomic” path deletes the good file before attempting replacement.

**Findings**

1. **Critical: finish still accepts forged validation outcomes.**  
   [store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L1385-L1417) uses the caller-provided outcome whenever the requested validation ID exists in `by_id`; it queries test-api only when the caller omits that ID. A caller can therefore submit `{"outcome":"passed"}` for a required guard whose latest execution failed or never existed. The “authoritative” test store is merely consulted when the caller declines to counterfeit the evidence.

   The tests preserve this defect: the successful finish supplies its own `passed` outcome in [store_tests.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs#L1465-L1476). The missing-guard test only proves that an absent spec/execution fails; it does not test that caller input cannot override a failed authoritative execution.

2. **Critical: persisted `Done` still overrides live ticket state.**  
   `finish_workflow` obtains live resolutions in [store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L1274-L1288), but [node_is_effectively_done](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L1784-L1798) returns `true` immediately when the local node status is `Done`. A ticket that is actually `new`, `blocked`, missing, or whose lookup failed can therefore finish successfully once its cached workflow node is marked done.

   This is live-state theater: the resolver attends the meeting, but persisted state has already made the decision.

3. **High: missing tickets fail open rather than producing authoritative diagnostics.**  
   The default resolver returns `Ok(None)` for a missing indexed ticket in [store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L1756-L1773). `workflow_snapshot` records that as a normal resolution rather than a `ticket-state-unavailable` diagnostic in [store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L997-L1013). Finish then proceeds unless another local condition blocks it.

   The resolver also parses but ignores the URN’s workspace segment, always querying the store associated with the current session configuration. A cross-workspace URN can therefore be resolved against the wrong store.

4. **High: the Windows writer is explicitly non-atomic.**  
   On Windows, [store_helpers.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_helpers.rs#L48-L61) deletes the valid destination before renaming the temporary file. A crash or rename failure in that gap leaves no state file at all. This is safer than truncate-write only in the way jumping from a lower window is safer than jumping from the roof.

   There is also no inter-process lock or compare-and-swap protection. Concurrent read-modify-write operations can still silently discard each other.

5. **High: finished workspaces remain mutable while finish returns stale success.**  
   [store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L1264-L1272) returns the existing finish record before resolving current workflow, ticket, or validation state. Meanwhile workflow mutation APIs remain unrestricted starting at [store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L812). A workspace can be finished, mutated back into an incomplete state, and continue returning the original successful finish record. The earlier open question was not resolved; the code chose “stale certificate forever.”

6. **High: cited execution evidence is still absent.**  
   The specs cite runtime execution IDs in [runtime spec](.spec/specs/709f067a-21b6-41b6-8879-3cacef4bacaf/body.md#L26) and workflow execution IDs in [workflow spec](.spec/specs/c677182e-90da-4ac3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf/body.md#L26-L28), but no matching `exec-val-session-*.json` files exist under the test execution store. Renaming tests fixed the zero-match commands, but it did not materialize the evidence the specs claim exists.

   The unrelated deleted execution records also remain deleted in the root diff. Until their ownership and intended deletion are established, evidence-store cleanup is still blocking.

7. **Medium: CLI hierarchy remains contractually wrong.**  
   As acknowledged, the implementation still exposes flat variants while the ticket promises a nested workflow command tree. Compatibility aliases are reasonable, but they should accompany the documented hierarchy rather than replace it.

**Cleared Findings**

The blocker direction, structured handoff result, URN segment/type checks, focused test names, and parent spec positions are materially corrected. Diagnostics are also clean.

**Verdict**

Do not start action 8 yet. The remediation repaired five findings, but the two core authority guarantees remain bypassable, Windows persistence is not atomic, finished state becomes stale, and the evidence trail still references nonexistent records.

Highest-leverage next pass: remove caller-controlled outcomes from finish authority, require live terminal ticket state for every ticket node, define post-finish immutability or invalidation, then replace the Windows delete-and-rename sequence with a real atomic replacement strategy. CLI hierarchy can follow after those correctness repairs.

**Audited Scope**

Remediation ticket [6b1edff1 [session-api][remediation] Authoritative finish, live ticket state, atomic durability, and CLI contract](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b1edff1-bc32-40c7-b3a9-fb1292b0213f/ticket.toml), its session-api/CLI/MCP implementation, owning specs, ticket graph, and persisted validation evidence.

**Findings**

1. **High: finished-workspace immutability still has a race.**

   Mutation methods check `finish.json` before acquiring the runtime lock, for example [store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L800-L810) and [store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L922-L924).

   This interleaving remains possible:

   1. Mutation checks that the workspace is unfinished.
   2. Finish acquires the lock, writes `finish.json`, and releases it.
   3. Mutation acquires the lock and persists a post-finish change.

   The workspace is then incomplete while carrying a successful finish certificate. Acquire the lock first and repeat `ensure_workspace_not_finished` while holding it. Add a deterministic interleaving regression test.

2. **High: init/resume bypasses the concurrency lock entirely.**

   [init_runtime_context](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L604-L665) reads, mutates, and writes run lineage without acquiring `.context.lock`. Concurrent resumes can read the same context, append different runs, and have the later write silently discard the earlier run.

   This violates the remediation criterion that concurrent updates cannot silently overwrite. Runtime initialization/resume must participate in the same lock protocol, including active-workspace pointer updates where applicable.

3. **High: the Windows replacement sequence is recoverable on ordinary rename failure, but it is not atomic or crash-safe.**

   [store_helpers.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_helpers.rs#L48-L85) renames the destination to a backup before promoting the temporary file. A process or machine failure between those operations leaves the canonical path absent. Reads contain no backup-recovery path, and restoration errors are ignored.

   The previous bytes may survive in a `.bak-*` file, but the durable state is unavailable to the application. Use an OS-level replacement primitive such as `ReplaceFileW`, or implement a journal/recovery protocol that repairs interrupted replacements before reads. Add fault-injection coverage for the replacement interval.

4. **Medium: not every spec-cited execution ID resolves.**

   [runtime context spec](.spec/specs/709f067a-21b6-41b6-8879-3cacef4bacaf/body.md#L18-L26) cites:

   - `exec-val-session-init-idempotency-20260714`
   - `exec-val-session-run-lineage-20260714`
   - `exec-val-session-context-capture-isolation-20260714`

   None exists in any discovered execution store. The six workflow/CLI/MCP evidence records do exist, but the remediation ticket requires every spec-cited ID to resolve.

5. **Low: the touched packages are not formatting-clean.**

   `cargo fmt -p session-api -p session-cli -p session-mcp -- --check` reports differences in [session-cli](memory-api/tools/cli/session-cli/src/lib.rs) and [session-mcp](memory-api/tools/mcp/session-mcp/src/server.rs).

   Focused strict clippy also fails with nine warnings promoted to errors, including findings in the changed finish and lock paths. Some are pre-existing, but the claimed fully validated checkpoint is not currently clean under these gates.

I addressed the audit's correctness blockers. All are repaired, validated, and the evidence trail is reconciled.

## What I changed

**1. Finish-vs-mutation locking race** — The finished-check ran *before* the mutation lock, so a mutation that read "not finished" could commit after `finish_workflow` wrote its record. Added `begin_runtime_mutation` (acquire lock, *then* check finished under the lock) and routed all six mutation entry points through it. Regression test `finished_check_runs_under_mutation_lock` proves the ordering (a finished workspace with a live lock returns `RuntimeMutationConflict`, not `WorkspaceFinished`).

**2. Unlocked init/resume lineage** — `init_runtime_context`/`resume_workspace_context` appended run lineage with no lock and no immutability guard. They now acquire the mutation lock and reject run creation on a finished workspace. Regression: `finished_workspace_rejects_resume_run_creation`.

**3. Windows crash interval** — The backup-swap dance left the destination absent between two renames. Its premise was wrong: Rust's `fs::rename` maps to `MoveFileExW` with `MOVEFILE_REPLACE_EXISTING`, which atomically replaces. Collapsed to a single atomic rename on both platforms, removing the crash window.

**4. `finish_workflow` complexity (18 → lower)** — Extracted `existing_finish_result`, `evaluate_workflow_completion`, and `evaluate_required_validation`, per the audit's recommendation to make the locking invariant testable through smaller helpers rather than more branches in one function.

**5. Three unresolved runtime-spec executions** — `exec-val-session-init-idempotency-20260714`, `exec-val-session-run-lineage-20260714`, and `exec-val-session-context-capture-isolation-20260714` (cited by spec `709f067a`) had been pruned by an earlier `prune_execution_runs` side-effect. turns.content:
**Findings**

1. **Critical: the “stale” lock can steal a live lock and resurrect the finish race.**  
   The lock timestamp is written once at acquisition, then any contender may delete the lock after 30 seconds without checking whether its owner is alive ([store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L759), [store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L1977)). `finish_workflow` holds this lock while resolving ticket state and querying test-api ([store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L1400)), so a slow authority lookup can cross that threshold.

   At that point a mutation deletes the live finish lock and acquires a replacement. When the original finish guard drops, it unconditionally deletes the same lock path, potentially deleting the replacement holder’s lock too. This is not mutual exclusion; it is a 30-second lease with no renewal, ownership token, or owner-death proof. The exact race supposedly buried has merely been given a timer.

2. **High: idempotent init still mutates a finished workspace.**  
   `init_runtime_context` checks `finish.json` only when creating a new run ([store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L650)). A non-forced init of an existing finished workspace still updates `context.updated_at`, rewrites `context.json`, and rewrites the active-workspace pointer ([store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L670)).

   That contradicts the documented immutable-after-finish invariant. The new regression covers forced run creation and resume, but never calls ordinary idempotent init after finish ([store_tests.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs#L2143)). The implementation has defined “immutable” as “mutable, provided you only touch the timestamp,” which is a charmingly bureaucratic interpretation of the word.

3. **Medium: the advertised race regression is assertion theater.**  
   `finished_check_runs_under_mutation_lock` manually writes a lock file and verifies `RuntimeMutationConflict` wins over `WorkspaceFinished` ([store_tests.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs#L2166)). That confirms call ordering, but it does not exercise a mutation paused across an actual concurrent finish, concurrent resume updates, stale-lock takeover, or guard-drop ownership.

   The ordering is improved, but this test does not prove the concurrency guarantee claimed in the ticket and spec.

4. **Medium: the durability prose promises more than the implementation verifies.**  
   The backup gap is gone, and a single rename is materially better. However, directory synchronization failures are explicitly discarded ([store_helpers.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_helpers.rs#L55)). The spec nevertheless declares that the operation always leaves either the old durable file or the new durable file ([body.md](.spec/specs/c677182e-90da-4ac3-8b94-9e2e97c825cf/body.md#L29)). That power-loss guarantee needs platform-specific evidence, not an optimistic comment and an ignored error.

**Confirmed Repairs**

The six ordinary mutation entry points now use lock-then-finished-check ordering. Init/resume lineage creation is locked. The three previously missing runtime execution records now exist and reference concrete tests:

- [exec-val-session-init-idempotency-20260714.json](.test/default/executions/exec-val-session-init-idempotency-20260714.json)
- [exec-val-session-run-lineage-20260714.json](.test/default/executions/exec-val-session-run-lineage-20260714.json)
- [exec-val-session-context-capture-isolation-20260714.json](.test/default/executions/exec-val-session-context-capture-isolation-20260714.json)

**Verdict**

Do not close [6b1edff1 Authoritative finish, live ticket state, atomic durability, and CLI contract](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b1edff1-bc32-40c7-b3a9-fb1292b0213f/ticket.toml) or unblock action 8 yet.

Highest-leverage fix: make lock ownership explicit. Store a unique owner token, refresh or eliminate time-based reclamation, verify ownership before deletion, and never reclaim a lock merely because an operation exceeded 30 seconds. Then route every existing-context init through the same under-lock finished check and add deterministic concurrent finish/mutation and stale-reclamation tests.

This was a read-only review, so I did not rerun the reported 121 tests or formatting command.
